candidates.why: Appears directly beneath 'Cost of goods sold' with a corresponding 'Cost of Revenues - Balancing value', indicating it is a component already embedded in the selected COGS row rather than a separate same-level add-on.

candidates.row_label: Depreciation and amortization of fixed assets
candidates.why: Separate operating D&A line in later years, but it looks like a broad operating total rather than a COGS-specific row; not safe to add to COGS separately from the selected 'Cost of goods sold' bucket.

variables.notes: Do not add any COGS D&A separately. The explicit D&A under COGS is presented as a sub-component of 'Cost of goods sold' and would double count if added. The later standalone 'Depreciation and amortization of fixed assets' is not clearly COGS-specific.

candidates.why: Repeated directly beneath 'Sales expenses', 'Administration expenses', and 'Research and development expenses' with corresponding balancing rows, indicating these D&A amounts are embedded components of the selected parent expense rows.

candidates.row_label: Amortization of Intangibles excluding Goodwill in Selling, General & Administrative Expenses
candidates.why: Although SG&A-specific by label, it sits directly under 'Sales expenses' with a balancing row, so it appears to be a sub-component already included in the selected SG&A bucket rather than a separate same-level line to add.

candidates.row_label: Depreciation and amortization of fixed assets
candidates.why: Standalone operating D&A line in later years appears separate from other operating expense rows, but it is not clearly limited to SG&A and may span broader operating functions, so adding it to XSGA risks misclassification or double counting.

variables.notes: For years with explicit Sales/Admin/R&D rows, the D&A lines are clearly sub-components already embedded in the selected parent rows, so they should not be added separately. The later standalone 'Depreciation and amortization of fixed assets' may be a broader operating total and cannot be confidently assigned only to XSGA from this excerpt.
